title: 10 best herbal tinctures for palpitation
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for palpitation are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs, which are used to treat various medical conditions, including irregular heartbeats or palpitations. These tinctures contain active compounds from herbs that help regulate heart function and reduce anxiety, which can trigger palpitations. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with palpitation include motherwort, hawthorn, and valerian root. Motherwort has been shown to reduce heart rate and ease anxiety, while hawthorn improves blood flow to the heart and valerian root promotes relaxation and sleep. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can manage their palpitations, reduce their dependence on prescription medications, and improve their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Midland hawthorn tincture helps with palpitation because it contains cardiotonic properties that can regulate and strengthen the heartbeat, making it an effective herbal remedy for irregular heart rhythms. The flavonoids and procyanidins present in hawthorn have been shown to dilate coronary blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing workload on the cardiovascular system. This, in turn, can help alleviate symptoms of palpitation, providing a natural and gentle solution for those seeking to maintain a healthy heart function.
remedy_properties: Antiarrhythmic: Midland hawthorn tincture contains cardioactive compounds like procyanidins and flavonoids, which help regulate heartbeats and reduce irregular palpitations.
Vasodilatory: The tincture's active constituents, including rutin and hyperoside, help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the strain on the heart, alleviating palpitations.
Cardiotonic: Midland hawthorn tincture contains cardiac glycosides, such as oleanolic acid, that strengthen heart contractions and regularize heart rhythms, reducing palpitations.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of the tincture, due to its rich flavonoid content, protect heart tissues from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing the risk of palpitations.
Anxiolytic: Midland hawthorn tincture contains compounds, such as vitexin and isovitexin, that have calming and relaxing effects, which may help alleviate anxiety-induced palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh Midland Hawthorn leaves, flowers, and berries, ensuring they are free from pesticides and environmental contaminants.
Chop the herbs finely and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it three-quarters full.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged, then secure the lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate the extraction process.

Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the spent herbs, and store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Recommended dosage is 30-60 drops, three times a day, for palpitation relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common hawthorn tincture helps with palpitation because it contains active compounds that have a positive impact on the cardiovascular system. These compounds include flavonoids, oligomeric procyanidins, and saponins, which work together to improve the function of the heart and blood vessels. Specifically, hawthorn has been shown to dilate coronary blood vessels, enhance blood flow to the heart, and regulate heartbeat, all of which can help alleviate symptoms of palpitation. Additionally, hawthorn has been found to have a mild sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, further helping to ease palpitations.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic: Hawthorn tincture strengthens and tones the heart muscle, improving cardiac function and regulating heartbeat, which is important for treating palpitation caused by heart weakness or insufficiency. This property is attributed to the flavonoids, procyanidins, and triterpene compounds present in hawthorn.

Vasodilatory: Hawthorn tincture dilates blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the workload on the heart, which is crucial for managing palpitation due to poor circulation or hypertension. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, procyanidins, and phenolic acids.

Antiarrhythmic: Hawthorn tincture helps regulate heart rhythm, reducing irregular heartbeats and palpitations, which is essential for treating palpitation caused by arrhythmias. The flavonoids and oligomeric procyanidins in hawthorn are believed to contribute to this property.

Anxiolytic: Hawthorn tincture has calming and sedative effects, which can help alleviate palpitation triggered by anxiety or stress. The active compounds responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Hawthorn tincture neutralizes free radicals and protects heart tissues from oxidative damage, which is important for maintaining overall cardiovascular health and preventing palpitation caused by oxidative stress. The antioxidant properties of hawthorn are attributed to its high flavonoid, procyanidin, and phenolic acid content.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried common hawthorn leaves and flowers into small pieces.
Combine herbs and 1 pint of 100-proof vodka in a clean jar, ensuring herbs are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid.
Store tincture in a glass dropper bottle and use 30-60 drops up to three times daily for palpitation relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort tincture helps with palpitation because it contains active compounds like alkaloids, flavonoids, and iridoid glycosides, which have a calming effect on the heart. These compounds help to regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations by increasing the supply of blood to the heart and improving its overall function. Additionally, motherwort has been shown to have a mild sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress, both of which can contribute to heart palpitations. By calming the heart and reducing anxiety, motherwort tincture can provide effective relief from palpitations and promote overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: Motherwort tincture helps regulate heartbeat rhythm and reduces palpitations by containing alkaloids like stachydrine and alkaloids, which have a relaxing effect on the heart muscle and can help stabilize heart rhythms.

Anxiolytic: It helps alleviate anxiety and nervous tension, which can contribute to palpitations, by containing iridoid glycosides, such as verbascoside, that have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Sedative: Motherwort tincture promotes relaxation, which can help reduce palpitations, due to its content of phenolic acids, including caffeic and rosmarinic acids, that have sedative properties.

Antispasmodic: It helps relieve smooth muscle spasms, including those in the heart, which can cause palpitations, by containing essential oil components like leonurine, which has antispasmodic properties.

Circulatory stimulant: Motherwort tincture improves blood flow and cardiovascular function, which can help reduce palpitations, by containing tannins, flavonoids, and other compounds that have a tonic effect on the circulatory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh motherwort herb (Leonurus cardiaca) or measure 1 ounce of dried herb.
Coarsely chop the herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, discarding the spent herb, and store the tincture in a tightly sealed glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tincture helps with palpitation because it contains passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, which has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various heart-related conditions. The herb is believed to have a calming effect on the body, helping to reduce anxiety and stress, which can often trigger palpitations. Maypop tincture may help regulate heart rhythm and promote relaxation, thus alleviating the symptoms of palpitation. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may also contribute to its cardioprotective effects, making it a valuable natural remedy for those experiencing heart palpitations.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: Maypop tincture contains alkaloids such as criocereine and chrysantherine, which help regulate heartbeat and reduce irregular heart rhythms associated with palpitation.
Anxiolytic: The flavonoids present in maypop, such as apigenin, have been shown to have anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce anxiety-induced palpitations.
Sedative: Maypop tincture contains compounds like 5-HTP and hordenine, which have sedative properties, helping to calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of palpitation.
Vasodilatory: Maypop contains alkaloids like lycorine, which act as vasodilators, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the strain on the heart and alleviating palpitations.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of maypop, due to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content, help protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall cardiovascular health and reducing the risk of palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 handfuls of ripe maypop (Passiflora incarnata) seeds and pulp, ensuring they are free from mold or damage.
Coarsely grind the maypop seeds and pulp, then combine them with 750 mL of 80-proof vodka in a clean jar, ensuring the mixture is fully submerged.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or muslin, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the resulting herbal tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, in a cool, dark place for up to a year, using 1-2 mL as needed for palpitation relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tincture helps with palpitation because it contains properties that can have a calming effect on the body. This herbal remedy is known for its ability to relax smooth muscles, which can help to regulate heart function and reduce palpitations. The tincture is made from the flowers and bark of the linden tree, which contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have a sedative effect on the nervous system. This can help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for palpitations. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension in the body, littleleaf linden tincture can help to manage palpitations and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antiarrhythmic: Littleleaf linden tincture contains constituents such as flavonoids and volatile oils that help regulate heart rhythm, which is important for treating palpitations.
Anxiolytic: The tincture has calming and sedative properties due to its active constituents like tiliroside and amino acids, helping to reduce anxiety-induced palpitations.
Vasodilatory: Littleleaf linden tincture contains compounds like flavonoids and essential oils that dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing palpitations caused by poor blood flow.
Antispasmodic: The tincture's antispasmodic properties, attributed to its active constituents like mucilage and volatile oils, help alleviate palpitations caused by muscle spasms in the heart.

Diuretic: Littleleaf linden tincture promotes diuresis due to its active compounds like flavonoids and tannins, aiding in the removal of excess fluids and reducing palpitations caused by fluid retention.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh littleleaf linden (Tilia cordata) leaves and flowers, ensuring they are dry and free of debris.
Chop or grind the herbs to increase surface area, placing them in a clean, dry jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent herbs. Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle, away from heat and light. Adults may take 30-60 drops of the tincture up to three times a day as needed for palpitations. Consult a healthcare provider for proper dosage and usage for children and those with medical conditions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tincture helps with palpitation because it contains natural compounds that have a calming effect on the heart. These compounds help to regulate the heartbeat and reduce symptoms of palpitation. The herb has been used traditionally to treat heart-related conditions, and its large leaves contain high levels of flavonoids, which have been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health. The tincture is made by soaking the leaves in alcohol, which extracts the active compounds and preserves them. When taken regularly, the tincture can help to stabilize the heartbeat and reduce the occurrence of palpitations. Additionally, large-leaved lime has anti-anxiety properties, which can help to alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of palpitations.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: Large-leaved lime tincture contains active constituents like flavonoids, which help regulate heartbeat and reduce irregular heart rhythms (palpitation) by controlling the electrical activity of the heart.

Vasodilatory: The tincture contains compounds such as limonoids that widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the strain on the heart, which can help alleviate palpitations caused by poor circulation or hypertension.
Anxiolytic: Large-leaved lime tincture has calming and relaxing properties due to its content of essential oils and flavonoids, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-induced palpitations.

Antioxidant: The tincture contains high levels of antioxidants like vitamin C and flavonoids, which help protect heart cells from oxidative damage, reducing the risk of palpitations caused by oxidative stress or heart disease.

Cardioprotective: Large-leaved lime tincture has active constituents that support overall cardiovascular health, such as magnesium and potassium, which can help maintain a regular heartbeat and prevent palpitations caused by electrolyte imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of large-leaved lime herb, then combine with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka and 1 cup of distilled water in a large jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean jar, discarding the plant material.
Add 1/4 cup of vegetable glycerin to the strained tincture, stirring well to mix.
Store the finished tincture in a cool, dark place for up to 1 year, taking 1-2 mL up to three times daily as needed for palpitations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tincture helps with palpitation because it contains valuable compounds like valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds help to regulate heart rhythm and reduce anxiety, thereby alleviating palpitations. Valerian tincture has been used traditionally for its sedative and hypnotic properties, making it an effective natural remedy for heart-related issues. Its ability to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality also contributes to its efficacy in managing palpitations. Overall, valerian tincture offers a holistic approach to addressing the root causes of palpitations, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural alternative to prescription medications.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Valerian tincture helps reduce anxiety, which is important for palpitation as anxiety can often trigger or worsen episodes of palpitation. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid and valepotriates.

Sedative: Valerian tincture has a sedative effect, which can help alleviate symptoms of palpitation by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid and isovaleramide.

Hypnotic: Valerian tincture can help improve sleep quality, which is important for palpitation as poor sleep can exacerbate symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid and valepotriates.

Antispasmodic: Valerian tincture can help alleviate muscle spasms, which is important for palpitation as spasms in the heart muscle can cause palpitations. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid and isovaleramide.

Cardiotonic: Valerian tincture can help regulate heart function, which is important for palpitation as irregular heart function can cause palpitations. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid and valtrates.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried valerian root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped valerian root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the solid plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place, where it will last for several years. Take 1-2 ml up to three times a day as needed for palpitations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European mistletoe tincture helps with palpitation because it contains active compounds that have a regulatory effect on the heart. Mistletoe has been found to contain cardioactive agents such as viscoside and beta-glycosides, which can help regulate heart function and reduce palpitations. Additionally, mistletoe has been shown to have a mild sedative effect, which can help calm the nervous system and potentially reduce the occurrence of palpitations brought on by stress or anxiety. However, it is important to note that mistletoe should only be taken under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can interact with certain medications and may not be suitable for everyone.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: European mistletoe tincture contains active constituents such as viscotoxins and glycosides that help regulate heart rhythm, which is important for treating palpitations caused by irregular heartbeats.

Cardioprotective: The antioxidant properties of European mistletoe tincture, due to flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the heart muscle from oxidative stress, reducing the risk of palpitations caused by heart disease.

Hypotensive: European mistletoe tincture has a mild blood pressure-lowering effect, which is important for managing palpitations associated with hypertension; this is attributed to the presence of glycosides and alkaloids.

Anxiolytic: European mistletoe tincture contains compounds with sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate palpitations caused by anxiety or stress; these properties are linked to the presence of various flavonoids and alkaloids.
Anti-inflammatory: European mistletoe tincture has anti-inflammatory properties due to its polysaccharide content, which can help reduce palpitations caused by systemic inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 400 grams of European mistletoe berries and leaves, ensuring they are fully ripe and disease-free.
Finely chop the mistletoe and place it in a clean, dry glass jar, then add 800 milliliters of 40-60 proof vodka or grain alcohol.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or muslin cloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid plant material.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, and use 1-2 milliliters up to three times daily for palpitation relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lily of the valley tincture helps with palpitation because it contains cardiac glycosides, which can help regulate the heart's rhythm and reduce palpitations. These compounds help to strengthen and regularize heartbeats, making it an effective herbal remedy for irregular heart rhythms and palpitations. Additionally, the herb has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, further contributing to the reduction of palpitations. It is important to note that while lily of the valley tincture can be helpful for palpitations, it should only be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can have toxic effects in high doses.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Containing cardiac glycosides like convallatoxin, lily of the valley tincture helps strengthen and regulate heartbeats, addressing palpitations caused by irregular heart rhythms.
Antiarrhythmic: The plant's ability to regulate heartbeat and treat arrhythmia is attributed to its antiarrhythmic properties, which can help stabilize heart function and alleviate palpitations.
Vasodilation: Lily of the valley tincture can dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and oxygen supply to the heart, thereby reducing palpitations caused by poor cardiac circulation.
Diuretic: Its diuretic properties help eliminate excess fluid and reduce the workload on the heart, which can help alleviate palpitations related to heart strain or fluid buildup.
Nervine: As a mild nervine, lily of the valley tincture can help soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety-induced palpitations and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lily of the valley flowers and leaves, ensuring a ratio of 2:1 (flowers to leaves) and a total of 2 ounces of herbal material.
Clean the herbs gently, avoiding moisture, and let them air dry for 12-24 hours.
Chop the herbs coarsely and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it three-quarters full.
Pour 100-proof vodka or rum over the herbs until they are completely submerged, then secure the jar with an airtight lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days, before straining and storing the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foxglove tincture helps with palpitation because it contains digoxin, a powerful compound that regulates the heart's rhythm. Palpitation is often caused by irregular heartbeats, and digoxin works by strengthening the heart's contractions and slowing down the heart rate. As a result, it can help reduce the frequency and intensity of palpitations, providing relief to those who experience this uncomfortable and sometimes frightening symptom. Moreover, foxglove tincture has been used for centuries as a natural remedy for various heart conditions, making it a popular alternative to conventional medications. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before taking any herbal supplement, especially for those with pre-existing heart conditions or those taking other medications.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic effect: Foxglove tincture helps strengthen and regulate heartbeats, treating palpitations by increasing the force of heart contractions, mainly due to the presence of cardiac glycosides like digoxin and digitoxin.

Antiarrhythmic effect: It helps maintain a normal heart rhythm by controlling irregular heartbeats, addressing palpitations through the inhibition of sodium and calcium channels, thanks to the action of cardiac glycosides.

Negative chronotropic effect: Foxglove tincture reduces heart rate, alleviating palpitations by blocking the beta-adrenergic receptors, which is facilitated by the presence of active constituents like digitoxin and gitoxin.

Vasodilatory effect: It widens blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the workload on the heart, thus helping to manage palpitations caused by poor circulation or hypertension, due to the presence of flavonoids and saponins.

Diuretic effect: Foxglove tincture promotes urine production, aiding in the elimination of excess fluids and reducing the strain on the heart, which can help alleviate palpitations, mainly due to the presence of cardiac glycosides and saponins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the foxglove flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pests, then rinse them gently in cool water.
Chop the foxglove herbs finely and fill a clean jar halfway with the chopped herbs, followed by adding 80-proof vodka until the jar is full, leaving about 1-inch of space at the top.
Seal the jar tightly and shake it vigorously for 1-2 minutes to ensure proper mixing, then store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the tincture using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out as much liquid as possible, and discard the leftover herbs.

Transfer the strained tincture to a clean, amber glass bottle, label it with the ingredients, preparation date, and expiration date (which is typically one year after preparation), and store it in a cool, dark place.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat palpitation with herbal tinctures is through the use of calming and normalizing herbs. For instance, Motherwort and Lemon Balm are well-known for their heart-regulating properties, while Hawthorn can help strengthen and tone the cardiovascular system. Valerian and Passionflower are also effective in reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate palpitations. When selecting tinctures, it's crucial to choose high-quality, organic herbs and follow dosage instructions carefully. Always consult with a healthcare practitioner before starting any new herbal regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to palpitation that are treated with herbal tinctures include arrhythmia, high blood pressure, and anxiety. Tinctures made from hawthorn, motherwort, and valerian root are often used to manage these conditions. Hawthorn helps to regulate heartbeat and lower blood pressure, while motherwort has a calming effect on the heart and nervous system. Valerian root is a natural sedative that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatment.
